# Gemini Enterprise API Explorer ‚Äî LLM Documentation

This document describes the Discovery Engine REST API surface used to whitelabel
Google's Gemini Enterprise: building your own chat UI, conversation history,
agent management, search, and NotebookLM features on top of the API.

Intended audience: LLMs and developers integrating these APIs. Everything below
has been verified against a live Gemini Enterprise project unless marked
otherwise.

## Base URL and authentication

- Base URL (global): `https://discoveryengine.googleapis.com/{version}`
- Base URL (regional): `https://{region}-discoveryengine.googleapis.com/{version}`
- Versions: `v1alpha` (most complete, includes agents + notebooks), `v1beta`, `v1`
- Auth: OAuth2 bearer token, e.g. `Authorization: Bearer $(gcloud auth print-access-token)`
- When using user credentials (not a service account), you MUST also send
  `X-Goog-User-Project: {projectNumber}` or calls fail with 403.
- Official REST reference: https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/enterprise/docs/reference/rest

## Resource hierarchy

```
projects/{projectNumber}/locations/{region}                          ‚Üê location
  /collections/{collection}                                          ‚Üê usually default_collection
    /engines/{engineId}                                              ‚Üê a Gemini Enterprise app
      /assistants/{assistant}                                        ‚Üê usually default_assistant
        /agents/{agentId}                                            ‚Üê custom agents
      /sessions/{sessionId}                                          ‚Üê conversations
        /assistAnswers/{assistAnswerId}                              ‚Üê per-turn answer details
      /servingConfigs/default_search                                 ‚Üê search/answer serving
      /completionConfig                                              ‚Üê autocomplete
    /dataStores/{dataStoreId}                                        ‚Üê knowledge sources
      /branches/default_branch/documents/{documentId}                ‚Üê ingested documents
  /notebooks/{notebookUuid}                                          ‚Üê NotebookLM (no collection/engine)
    /sources/{sourceUuid}
    /audioOverviews/{audioOverviewId}
```

## Sessions (conversation history)

| Method | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| GET | `{engine}/sessions` ‚Äî list; supports `pageSize`, `filter` (e.g. `userPseudoId = "user-1"`), `orderBy` (e.g. `update_time desc`) |
| GET | `{engine}/sessions/{id}` ‚Äî one session incl. turns; `includeAnswerDetails=true` for full history |
| POST | `{engine}/sessions` ‚Äî body: `{displayName, userPseudoId}` |
| PATCH | `{engine}/sessions/{id}?updateMask=displayName` ‚Äî rename a conversation |
| POST | `{engine}/sessions/{id}:addContextFile` ‚Äî body: `{fileName, mimeType, fileContents}` (base64); "attach file to chat" |
| GET | `{engine}/sessions/{id}/assistAnswers/{answerId}` ‚Äî full answer details: citations, grounding, references |
| DELETE | `{engine}/sessions/{id}` |

## Assistant / Chat

| Method | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| POST | `{assistant}:streamAssist` ‚Äî chat, streamed JSON chunks |
| POST | `{assistant}:assist` ‚Äî chat, single response |
| GET | `{assistant}` ‚Äî assistant config |
| GET | `{engine}/assistants` ‚Äî list assistants |
| PATCH | `{assistant}?updateMask=generationConfig` ‚Äî customize: `generationConfig.systemInstruction.additionalSystemInstruction` sets a custom persona/tone (key whitelabel API); also web grounding and enabled tools |

### streamAssist request body

```json
{
  "query": { "text": "user message" },
  "session": "projects/.../engines/.../sessions/-",
  "answerGenerationMode": "NORMAL",
  "agentsConfig": { "agent": "projects/.../agents/AGENT_ID" }
}
```

- `session` ending in `/sessions/-` auto-creates a session; the new session
  resource name is returned in `sessionInfo.session` ‚Äî reuse it to continue.
- Response is a JSON array of chunks; assistant text accumulates in
  `answer.replies[].groundedContent.content.text`.
- Trivial greetings may return `answer.state: "SKIPPED"` with
  `assistSkippedReasons: ["NON_ASSIST_SEEKING_QUERY_IGNORED"]`.
- `agentsConfig.agent` routes to a specific agent (observed: routing may still
  answer from the default assistant persona depending on deployment state).

## Agents

| Method | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| GET | `{assistant}/agents` ‚Äî list (for an agent picker UI) |
| GET | `{assistant}/agents/{id}` |
| POST | `{assistant}/agents` ‚Äî create (ADK, Dialogflow, or low-code; see below) |
| PATCH | `{assistant}/agents/{id}` ‚Äî update |
| DELETE | `{assistant}/agents/{id}` |
| POST | `{assistant}/agents/{id}:requestAgentReview` ‚Äî empty body |
| POST | `{assistant}/agents/{id}:rejectAgent` ‚Äî body: `{"rejectionReason": "..."}` |
| POST | `{assistant}/agents/{id}:withdrawAgent` ‚Äî empty body |

### Agent review state machine (verified live)

```
PRIVATE (draft) --requestAgentReview--> DISABLED (pending review)
DISABLED --rejectAgent (admin, requires rejectionReason)--> PRIVATE
DISABLED --withdrawAgent (author)--> PRIVATE
DISABLED --admin approval (console; API shape unknown)--> deployed/shared
```

`rejectAgent` returns `FAILED_PRECONDITION "Agent is not in DISABLED state"`
if the agent is not pending review.

### Low-code agents (lowCodeAgentDefinition ‚Äî NOT in public docs, verified working)

`agents.create` accepts a `lowCodeAgentDefinition` with a graph of LLM agent
nodes. Verified round-trip on v1alpha:

```json
{
  "displayName": "My Agent",
  "description": "What this agent does",
  "lowCodeAgentDefinition": {
    "nodes": [
      {
        "id": "root_agent",
        "displayName": "My Agent",
        "llmAgentNode": {
          "model": "gemini-3.5-flash",
          "instruction": "System prompt for this node",
          "subAgentIds": ["sub_agent_1"],
          "selectedTools": { "tool": [{ "name": "googleSearch" }] }
        }
      },
      {
        "id": "sub_agent_1",
        "displayName": "Sub Agent",
        "llmAgentNode": {
          "description": "Routing description",
          "model": "gemini-3.5-flash",
          "instruction": "System prompt",
          "selectedTools": { "tool": [{ "name": "googleSearch" }] }
        }
      }
    ],
    "rootAgentId": "root_agent"
  }
}
```

Gotchas (all verified):
- Created agents are PRIVATE drafts. `nodes` = draft; `deployedNodes` /
  `deployedRootAgentId` are OUTPUT-ONLY and cannot be patched. Deployment
  happens via the review/approval workflow (admin approves in console).
- PATCH requires re-sending `displayName` AND
  `lowCodeAgentDefinition.draftDisplayName` every time, or it 400s.
- `:deploy` and `:publish` verbs exist on the agent resource but their working
  request shapes are undocumented; `:publish` returns 200 without deploying.
- ADK agents instead use `adkAgentDefinition.provisionedReasoningEngine.reasoningEngine`
  pointing at a Vertex AI reasoning engine.

## Search & Answers (non-chat serving path)

| Method | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| POST | `{engine}/servingConfigs/default_search:search` ‚Äî body: `{query, pageSize}` |
| POST | `{engine}/servingConfigs/default_search:answer` ‚Äî body: `{query: {text}}`; generated answer + citations |
| POST | `{engine}/servingConfigs/default_search:streamAnswer` ‚Äî streaming variant |
| POST | `{engine}/completionConfig:completeQuery` ‚Äî body: `{query}`; autocomplete |

## Engine & Data Stores

| Method | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| GET | `{engine}` ‚Äî engine config; `dataStoreIds[]` = data stores connected to this engine |
| PATCH | `{engine}?updateMask=dataStoreIds` ‚Äî attach/detach data stores |
| GET | `{collection}/engines` ‚Äî list engines (discover engine IDs) |
| GET | `{collection}/dataStores` ‚Äî list all data stores in collection |
| GET | `{collection}/dataStores/{id}` ‚Äî one data store's config |
| GET | `{collection}/dataStores/{id}/branches/default_branch/documents` ‚Äî browse documents |
| GET | `.../documents/{docId}` ‚Äî one document (resolve citations to sources) |

## NotebookLM (v1alpha; lives under locations, not engines)

| Method | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| GET | `{location}/notebooks:listRecentlyViewed?pageSize=N` ‚Äî per-user notebook list |
| GET | `{location}/notebooks/{uuid}` ‚Äî one notebook (title, emoji, sources) |
| POST | `{location}/notebooks` ‚Äî body: `{title}` (only writable field) |
| POST | `{location}/notebooks:batchDelete` ‚Äî body: `{names: [full resource names]}` |
| POST | `{location}/notebooks/{uuid}/sources:batchCreate` ‚Äî add sources |
| POST | `{location}/notebooks/{uuid}/sources:batchDelete` ‚Äî body: `{names: [...]}` |
| POST | `{location}/notebooks/{uuid}/audioOverviews` ‚Äî generate Audio Overview (podcast) |
| DELETE | `{location}/notebooks/{uuid}/audioOverviews/{id}` |
| POST | `{location}/notebooks/{uuid}:share` ‚Äî body: `{accountAndRoles: [{email, role}], notifyViaEmail}` |

### Source upload variants (sources:batchCreate)

Each entry in `userContents[]` is exactly ONE of:

```json
{ "textContent":  { "sourceName": "display name", "content": "raw text" } }
{ "webContent":   { "sourceName": "display name", "url": "https://..." } }
{ "videoContent": { ... } }
{ "googleDriveContent": { ... } }
{ "agentspaceContent":  { ... } }
```

### Audio overview generation

```json
{
  "generationOptions": {
    "episodeFocus": "what the hosts should focus on",
    "languageCode": "en",
    "sourceIds": [{ "id": "source-uuid" }]
  }
}
```

Generation is async ‚Äî poll `GET {location}/notebooks/{uuid}` until the audio
overview appears. `sourceIds` is optional (defaults to all sources).

### NotebookLM web app URL

`https://notebooklm.cloud.google.com/{region}/?project={projectNumber}`
(project- and region-specific; admins distribute this URL to users).

## General gotchas

- Use the project NUMBER (not project ID) in paths and `X-Goog-User-Project`.
- Notebook and source IDs are UUIDs; engine/data store IDs are slugs; session
  and agent IDs are numeric strings.
- PATCH endpoints generally want an explicit `?updateMask=...`.
- Streaming endpoints (`:streamAssist`, `:streamAnswer`) return a JSON array
  that arrives incrementally ‚Äî parse leniently until the stream completes.
- In zsh, `$VAR:streamAssist` mangles the URL (`:s` is a history modifier) ‚Äî
  always write `${VAR}:streamAssist` in shell scripts.
